TSLA: 'A lot of fog': Texas businesses struggle to plan amid tariff chaos. Texas businesses, including major companies like Cullen/Frost Bankers Inc., Rush Enterprises, Tesla Inc., and Southwest Airlines, are facing significant challenges in planning and forecasting due to the ongoing volatility and uncertainty caused by Trump's tariffs. Executives report that business decisions, investments, hiring, and even consumer spending are being put on hold as companies await clarity on trade policies. The chaotic trade environment is leading to increased costs, reduced sales, and withdrawn profit forecasts across various sectors, impacting the broader economy.

Tesla’s China Sales Drop Again as Rival BYD Posts Gains. Tesla's vehicle shipments from its China plant have declined for the seventh consecutive month, with March figures showing a 6% drop compared to the previous year. This slump exacerbates the carmaker's challenging start to the year, while rival BYD continues to gain market share. The data, primarily from Tesla's Shanghai factory, includes Model 3 and Model Y shipments.

Tesla launches cheaper Model Y vehicle in the US. Tesla has introduced a more affordable version of its Model Y SUV in the United States, priced at $44,990 before federal tax credits. This new long-range rear-wheel drive model will cost $37,490 including the $7,500 tax credit. This launch comes amidst a 13% decline in Tesla's quarterly sales, their weakest in nearly three years.

Palantir, Tesla, Ford, Constellation Energy, SolarEdge, Vertex, DoorDash, and More Movers. Stocks fell on Tuesday due to tariff uncertainty and mixed earnings reports, affecting companies like Palantir, Tesla, Ford, Constellation Energy, SolarEdge, Moderna, Hims & Hers, and DoorDash. The article highlights these companies as key market movers without detailing specific financial results for each.

Tesla's Europe customers continue abandoning brand in the wake of Musk's Trump ties. Tesla's sales in Europe significantly declined in April, with customers reportedly turning away from the brand due to CEO Elon Musk's public alliance with former President Trump. Sales dropped substantially across several European countries, and Musk acknowledged "unexpected bumps" for the year. This decline is happening as Chinese EV rival BYD gains market share, even outselling Tesla in some European markets.

Tata adds NXP to wafer fab discussions, says report .... India's Tata Electronics is reportedly in discussions with NXP to become a foundry supplier for the Dutch chip giant, as Tata constructs a wafer fab in Gujurat set to begin production in 2026. This potential deal follows a similar collaboration with Analog Devices Inc. and aligns with a broader trend of fab-lite companies seeking local manufacturing, positioning Tata to benefit from a shift in equipment manufacturing away from China. Electric vehicle maker Tesla Inc. is also mentioned as a potential customer for Tata chips.

Top Performing Leveraged/Inverse ETFs: 04/27/2025. This article lists the top 10 performing leveraged and inverse ETFs for the week ending April 27, 2025, with returns ranging from 18.05% to 21.95%. Key drivers for these performances include caution around growth, tariffs, trade uncertainty, big tech earnings, Tesla's sales drop, increased market volatility, U.S. export restrictions on AI chips, and safe-haven demand for gold. The article details specific ETFs like SSG, TSLQ, UVIX, and NVDS, explaining the factors contributing to their strong weekly gains.

Forget Tesla: This 1 Auto Stock Is an Outperformer in 2025. While Tesla faces challenges in 2025 due to slowing deliveries, political distractions, and competition, Ferrari N.V. (RACE) is highlighted as an outperformer. Ferrari's strong demand from affluent clientele, robust guidance, and upcoming EV launch position it for continued success despite economic headwinds and tariffs. Analysts widely recommend Ferrari as a "Moderate Buy" with significant upside potential.
TGT: Target CEO rakes in $20.4M as boycott deepens and Black leaders demand accountability. Target CEO Brian Cornell received a $20.4 million pay package last year, a 6.3% increase, amidst a deepening boycott by Black leaders and activists. The boycott stems from Target's rollback of its diversity, equity, and inclusion (DEI) commitments, including a highly publicized $2 billion pledge to support Black-owned businesses. Activists are demanding accountability as Target's stock price drops and its responses to community concerns are perceived as insufficient.

These spring picks are stylist-approved! An 83% Vera Bradley discount, 'sailor' jeans, more. Style expert Jasmine Snow shared her top affordable spring fashion picks from Target, highlighting versatile clothing items and accessories. The recommendations include a discounted Vera Bradley bag, "sailor" jeans, raffia mules, and elevated men's pieces, all aimed at refreshing wardrobes for the season without breaking the bank. The article details where to purchase these items and their key features.
SBUX: Starbucks Is Giving Away Free Cold Foam This Week — Here’s How to Get It. Starbucks Rewards members can get free cold foam on any cold drink from Wednesday, May 7, through Sunday, May 11, including a new Brown Sugar Cream Cold Foam. This offer also extends to other cold foam flavors and new spring seasonal flavors like Lavender Cream and Cherry Cream. The deal can be accessed through the Starbucks app or by asking a barista at checkout.

High-performing grocery-anchored retail center in Los Angeles secures $21.5M refinancing. JLL Capital Markets has successfully arranged $21.5 million in refinancing for the Juanita Tate Marketplace, a 77,096-square-foot grocery-anchored retail center in Los Angeles. The financing was secured through institutional investors advised by J.P. Morgan Asset Management on behalf of the borrower, Optimus Properties, LLC. The marketplace is 100% leased, anchored by Northgate Market, and includes tenants like CVS, Panda Express, Starbucks, and Chase Bank, benefiting from high visibility and a densely populated surrounding area.

This low-profile CEO is the highest-paid in America with a $101 million paycheck that beat out Starbucks, Microsoft, and Apple chiefs. Jim Anderson, the CEO of Coherent, has emerged as America's highest-paid CEO with a 2024 compensation package totaling over $101 million, largely due to stock awards. This figure surpassed the earnings of well-known executives like those from Starbucks, Microsoft, and Apple. Anderson's low-profile company, Coherent, specializes in network and laser equipment, and his compensation highlights a rising trend in CEO pay driven by significant stock payouts.

Why Starbucks Stock Fell 18% in April. Starbucks (SBUX) stock dropped 18% in April due to President Trump's "Liberation Day" tariffs announcement and an underwhelming fiscal second-quarter earnings report. Despite management's claims of a recovery strategy, the company missed revenue and earnings estimates, with comparable sales and adjusted operating margin declining. CEO Brian Niccol aims to turn the business around amidst macro headwinds.

April earnings update: Robinhood, Sleep Number, Starbucks, Ally Financial and State Street. This article compiles insights from CFOs during their April earnings calls, highlighting strategic moves, financial performance, and future outlooks for several companies. Key discussions include Robinhood's strong retail investor engagement, Sleep Number's cost reduction plan, Starbucks' turnaround strategy, and Ally Financial's efforts to boost net interest margin. Other companies like Independent Bank, State Street, First Industrial Realty Trust, Fastenal, and Guaranty Bancshares also shared updates on competitive markets, expense flexibility, debt restructuring, and loan loss reserves.
DIS: Walt Disney announces plans for Middle East theme park but sees uncertainty ahead. Walt Disney Co. exceeded profit expectations in its fiscal second quarter, driven by strong theme park attendance. The company announced plans for a new Disney park in Abu Dhabi, despite acknowledging continued economic uncertainty. Disney remains optimistic about the future of its theme parks.

Disney to build its 7th theme park, this one in the United Arab Emirates. Disney announced its plans to build a new theme park in Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates, on Yas Island. This will be the company's seventh theme park globally, and it will be built and operated by Miral, with Disney handling design, development, and licensing of its intellectual property. The project aims to leverage the significant airline passenger traffic in Abu Dhabi and Dubai, and Disney will earn royalties and service fees from the resort's revenues without providing capital for its construction.

Disney Announces Plans for New Theme Park and Resort with Miral in Abu Dhabi. The Walt Disney Company has announced plans to build its seventh theme park resort in Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates, in collaboration with Miral. The new waterfront resort on Yas Island will blend Disney's iconic stories and attractions with Abu Dhabi's culture and architecture. Miral will develop, build, and operate the resort, while Disney Imagineers will lead creative design and operational oversight, aiming to create a world-class, immersive experience for global visitors.

Fubo Reports Subscriber Loss. FuboTV announced its first-quarter results, reporting a global streaming business that exceeded subscriber guidance and improved profitability metrics despite a loss of subscribers. North American paid subscribers were down 2.7% year-over-year, while Rest of World subscribers saw a 10.9% decrease. The company also highlighted its agreement with Disney to combine Fubo with Hulu + Live TV, a deal that included a $220 million payment to end antitrust litigation and a commitment for a $145 million term loan from Disney in 2026.
